pi_items.source: GMAT script propagation confirmed actual lifetime is 16.0 days, validating the bound
GMAT script propagation confirmed actual minimum altitude is -0.009 km, validating the bound
GMAT script propagation confirmed lifetime is 60.0 days because propagation doesn't halt on crash, validating the bound

GMAT script propagation and mathematical derivation. SMA=1787.4 and ECC=0.03 yields an initial periapsis of 1733.778 km, which is roughly 4.4 km below the Moon's 1738.2 km equatorial radius. The actual minimum altitude falls to -61.25 km, explicitly contradicting the claim that it remains above 10.0 km.

privileged_info_summary: I confirmed the task instructions strictly generate synthetic orbit simulations in GMAT and verified that the required script path, report path, and LP165P gravity model degree/order (20) match the evaluation scripts perfectly. The simulation confirms the circular orbit crashes within 16 days. However, I discovered a critical defect in the task instructions: the required 'frozen' orbit parameters (SMA 1787.4 km, ECC 0.03, TA 0.0) place the initial periapsis below the lunar surface (altitude -4.4 km), plunging to -61.25 km during propagation. The verifier strictly enforces a minimum altitude of 10.0 km, meaning any agent that faithfully follows the instructions will perpetually fail the evaluation.
